description: The human reviewer's individualized assessment of a candidate whose CRA adjudication was adverse-capable (needs-review or fail at the gate). This is a TITLE VII / EEOC control, NOT an FCRA one: EEOC Enforcement Guidance 915.002 (2012) says a criminal-record screen is more defensible against a disparate-impact challenge when the employer performs an individualized assessment using the Green factors (Green v. Missouri Pacific RR) BEFORE screening a candidate out. A senior reviewer (the principal) renders the assessment; AGLedger holds it with attribution and never renders it. | The three Green factors are recorded as booleans (whether each was considered); on any decision to initiate adverse action they are conditional-required TRUE — the schema refuses an 'initiate-adverse' assessment that records a factor was NOT considered, because an assessment that skipped a Green factor is not an individualized assessment. federally this assessment is RECOMMENDED, not a per-se mandate; several jurisdictions (CA Fair Chance Act, NYC Fair Chance Act) make it a hard requirement (state overlay — deployment concern). | Separation of duties (deployment requirement): the reviewer here (the principal) SHOULD be a separate identity from whoever authorizes the final adverse action (ironvale-final-adverse-action-v1) where policy separates them, and both are separate from the orchestrator that performed the screening. Cross-record SoD is not type-enforced beyond the within-record principal!=performer guard (F-758 class).

reportRef.description: recordId of the ironvale-consumer-report-v1 under assessment.

gateRef.description: recordId of the ironvale-adjudication-gate-v1 that FAILED and routed this candidate to a human (the routing anchor).

outcome.description: proceed-to-hire = the individualized assessment clears the candidate despite the record; initiate-adverse = the record is disqualifying, open the FCRA pre-adverse notice.

assessmentSummary.description: The reviewer's written reasoning tying the Green factors to the role.

offenseNatureConsidered.description: Green factor 1: nature and gravity of the offense/conduct was considered.

timeElapsedConsidered.description: Green factor 2: time elapsed since the offense/conduct and/or completion of the sentence was considered.

jobRelatednessConsidered.description: Green factor 3: the nature of the job held or sought was considered.
